title: calycadenia multiglandulosa var. cephalotes
intro: Calycadenia multiglandulosa var. cephalotes, also known as the San Diego bellflower, is a rare and endemic flowering plant native to the coastal areas of southern California. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and flavonoid content. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and possibly exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Calycadenia multiglandulosa var. cephalotes can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its availability and usage are mostly limited to traditional and local applications.
